title: shift()
subtitle: Remove from the beginning of an array
goal: Learn to remove the first element of an array using shift().
explanation: shift() removes the first element of an array.
After it is removed, all the other elements shift to the left.
The method changes the original array.
It works with the beginning of an array.
shift() is useful when working with queues.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: Imagine a line at a store. shift() removes the person standing first in line.

remember: It works with the beginning of an array.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Beginners confuse shift() with pop(). shift() works with the beginning of an array, while pop() works with the end.
